metadata.purpose: Canonical Wave 3 execution ledger and readiness audit.
notes: Wave 3 planning docs were audited against repository reality before execution began.
Wave 3 scope was narrowed to unfinished work only.
Tasks already owned and completed by Wave 2 were removed from the active Wave 3 execution map and recorded as satisfied prerequisites instead.
Several Wave 3 dependency labels were non-canonical lane names rather than executable tasks; those dependencies were repaired into exact repository-backed prerequisites.

After execution order 095, Wave 3 entered a canonical product-truth hardening phase that is tracked in docs/nexus-loop-productization-canonical-block.json and governed by docs/operating-system/wave3-product-truth-hardening-plan.md.
Wave 4 readiness is gated on closing critical Wave 3 product-truth hardening gaps on the live Nexus site at http://127.0.0.1:4011/.
Wave 3 also carries a canonical parallel planning track for AI-guided onboarding intelligence at docs/operating-system/wave3-onboarding-intelligence-planning-track.md.

After the repeated-loop closure lane reached trueGreen, Wave 3 opened a new canonical live closure lane for existing-project upload verification at docs/operating-system/wave3-existing-project-upload-closure-track.md.

After the existing-project upload closure lane reached trueGreen, Wave 3 opened a canonical planning track for live class sweep contract definition at docs/operating-system/wave3-live-class-sweep-track.md before any broader cross-class sweep execution or Wave 4 planning may begin.

After the live class sweep rerun reached complete-clean and the Wave 3 reopener closure pass closed W3-PTH-012 through W3-PTH-016 on 2026-05-18, Wave 3 product-truth hardening stopped blocking Wave 4 planning.

Wave 4 planning is now governed by docs/operating-system/wave4-end-state-definition.md, which defines Minimum Believable Nexus as a non-negotiable planning baseline rather than treating Wave 4 as boundary, GTM, and owner shell only.

wave3ProductTruthHardening.visualCapabilityCompletionRule: Any capability that claims to create a visual outcome must end as a real visual product surface inside Nexus on 127.0.0.1:4011, not at metadata, schema, regions, preview payloads, bullets, summaries, validation output, or internal renderer state.

onboardingIntelligence.problemStatement: Onboarding still feels too generic and weakens question quality, flow steering, artifact quality, trust, and downstream loop quality.

liveClassSweepPlanning.problemStatement: Nexus now needs a deterministic live class sweep contract so future browser-driven class verification produces canonical blockers instead of agent interpretation drift.
requiredOutputs: wave4 prohibition rules while sweep remains unclosed

activeTask.title: Create-start reliability on failing create/upload handoff paths
scope: failing paths recorded in the canonical failure ledger only

nonNegotiableRequirements: real visible product build progression on live surfaces
runtime and packaging preparation per product class
product-owned backend skeleton generated with the first frontend skeleton and grown with future Build changes
generated product runtime package with product-owned dependencies, install plan, run command, and preview target before standalone artifact or release claims
releaseable product state and continuation loop
standalone releasable product artifact before Nexus claims a user-ready product can be released
no meaningful backend/core/runtime progression without visible surface progression and live browser verification in parallel
external product intelligence must be integrated through canonical lane/task structure rather than copied or bolted on
structural UI evolution must pass through coherent Figma-backed design planning
Figma integration is a real Wave 4 execution capability with explicit structural-pass threshold rules
Figma remains design support and visual-planning aid only; Nexus itself must own generation, runtime, release, and continuation capability internally
wave4PlanningTrack.interpretationRule: Wave 4 may not be framed or closed truthfully as boundary, GTM, and owner shell only. Those layers remain subordinate to Minimum Believable Nexus core execution.
wave4PlanningTrack.workRule: From this point forward, meaningful Nexus capabilities must be built and verified as coupled backend-to-surface product truth: build capability, expose it visibly, test live, observe real behavior, repair or open a canonical task if broken, rerun live, and only then continue.
wave4PlanningTrack.delegationRule: From this point forward, Nexus development should actively use bounded delegation to execution workers when it truthfully accelerates closure, while keeping one main orchestrator as the sole authority for architecture direction, task ordering, closure decisions, rerun discipline, canonical write-back, lane promotion, and wave transitions.

postWave4PreparedContinuationLanes.activationRule: may begin only after active Wave 4 core tasks no longer select unresolved W4-MBN work ahead of it

preparedTasks.title: Define canonical learning system contract
Define adaptive onboarding agent contract
Implement deep adaptive learning decision impact
Connect learning signals to generation decisions
Replace fixed 3-question onboarding with adaptive intake flow
Connect learning signals to adaptive onboarding question selection
Implement shell-level provider-backed canonical onboarding agent runtime
realityAuditNotes: The onboarding runtime now exposes visible provider selection, provider identity, canonical Nexus rule enforcement, and continuity-safe provider-backed QA restore.
Repository reality still does not show real external OpenAI or Anthropic onboarding API call paths, provider auth, streaming, retry/backoff, rate-limit handling, failover, or token/cost accounting.
W4-INTAKE-004 may stay trueGreen only as shell-level provider runtime closure; real external provider integration requires a separate task.

preparedTasks.title: Inject smart onboarding agent truth into canonical downstream system surfaces
Connect onboarding agent to real external model provider APIs
residualProviderAvailabilityNotes: OpenAI live onboarding provider path is verified on the live QA route with provider-backed-live runtime truth, token accounting, and continuity-safe refresh.
Anthropic is now available in this environment and was used for live depth-gate verification on 2026-05-21.

preparedTasks.title: Stream live provider responses through the onboarding conversation surface
Add retry recovery and provider failover continuity to onboarding runtime
Harden onboarding provider runtime to production-safe operation
Expose user-facing provider, model, and intelligence controls on the onboarding agent
preparedTasks.liveClosureProof: A live QA rerun on 127.0.0.1:4011 with qaOnboardingAvailability=anthropic-missing-key surfaced Anthropic as a disabled option with visible ANTHROPIC_API_KEY availability truth, kept OpenAI active, and preserved the same unavailable-option truth after refresh.

preparedTasks.title: Enforce minimum real product-conversation depth before understanding closure
Enforce co-founder-style exploratory product reasoning before understanding closure
Add floating cross-Nexus product-conversation agent presence
Implement post-onboarding clarification and correction loop
Bring competitor and comparable-product intelligence into the live agent dialogue
liveClosureProof: Comparable-product intelligence is live through the real project companion backend path on project-backed sessions.
The non-QA shell now restores a project-backed /loop surface with the floating companion visibly reachable from stored project/session context.
The non-QA shell now restores a project-backed /understanding surface with the floating companion visibly reachable from stored project/session context.
The restored /loop surface preserves the floating companion and comparable-product transcript through refresh continuity.

preparedTasks.title: Convert external product-conversation patterns into Nexus product-family wrapper packs
liveClosureProof: The live marketplace onboarding route now opens with a two-sided marketplace fork, keeps the second turn in matching/trust/closure space, preserves marketplace-specific summary and missing truth, and survives refresh without stale preview leakage.

The live logistics onboarding route now accepts a natural mixed first-turn actor answer, advances into scan/map/route ambiguity, keeps logistics-specific summary and missing truth, and survives refresh without stale session leakage.

The live CRM onboarding route now opens with lead-ownership / follow-up framing, keeps the second turn in ownership/reminder/next-step space, preserves CRM-specific summary and missing truth, and survives refresh without stale session leakage.

The live booking onboarding route now opens with patient-vs-clinic scheduling framing, keeps the second turn in availability/approval/cancellation space, preserves booking-specific summary and missing truth, and survives refresh without stale session leakage.

The live dashboard onboarding route now opens with operator decision-making framing, keeps the second turn in anomaly/decision/drill-down space, preserves dashboard-specific summary and missing truth, and survives refresh without stale session leakage.

preparedTasks.title: Derive live conversational tone and pacing rules from external product intelligence
liveClosureProof: The live storefront onboarding route now opens with a momentum-based commerce question, keeps summary and missing blocks human, treats `לקוח שלי` as unresolved actor truth instead of hardening it, and survives refresh without losing storefront structure.

The live CRM onboarding route now keeps `המשתמש זה אני` in the correct body/person on visible Nexus copy, and the `/understanding` route now carries the same CRM truth into `אתה בעצמך` and `עבורך` without reverting to stale landing-page expectation.

The live `/understanding` screen now preserves the same product-family truth, human pacing, and non-robotic summary language after restore instead of leaking stale artifact expectations or system phrasing.

preparedTasks.title: Define conversation-first entry foundation contract and route ownership
liveClosureProof: Repository and active-route reality still prove `/` resolves to `create` and the first-run path still depends on visible create state before onboarding begins.
Canonical state now releases the conversation-first entry pivot ahead of `W4-GEN-002` instead of forcing the work into the wrong task.
The replacement-first, no-broken-front-door, and Figma-threshold rules are now locked before any front-door demotion can happen.

preparedTasks.title: Run Figma-backed primary entry shell structural pass
liveClosureProof: The Figma artifact now defines a Nexus-grade conversation-first front door instead of a generic chat shell.
The structural pass explicitly covers hidden bootstrap, progressive reveal of understanding support, and migration-safe fallback before any route demotion.
The old create/onboarding front door remains protected until later live implementation proof exists.

preparedTasks.title: Implement conversation-first front door with hidden project/session/bootstrap
liveClosureProof: The `/` route now opens on a Nexus-grade conversation-first composer instead of a visible project-create ritual.
A real first message submit now auto-creates the draft + onboarding session and opens the live CRM / follow-up onboarding question on `http://127.0.0.1:4011/onboarding?nexusState=...`.
A real reload on that live onboarding route now restores the same session-backed onboarding conversation and summary truth instead of collapsing to `אין onboarding פעיל לשחזור`.

The restore proof held after promoting top-level audience copy helpers and after hardening `persistFlowState()` so early restore writes preserve the live onboarding project/session/conversation truth.

preparedTasks.title: Harden durability, idempotency, restore, and stale-state isolation on the new entry path
liveClosureProof: A fresh `/` entry now survives reload as `create` without rehydrating an older onboarding session, project id, or companion truth.
The conversation-first send path now disables the send and file-picker controls immediately so a second retry click cannot create a second canonical first turn while bootstrap is in flight.
A real first-message submit on `http://127.0.0.1:4011/` still opens a live onboarding session and the expected CRM / follow-up first question.

Refresh on that live onboarding route now shows an honest restore shell first and then rehydrates the same session-backed question instead of flashing a fake generic onboarding conversation or dropping to `אין onboarding פעיל לשחזור`.
The restored onboarding route keeps the same `sessionId`, same project id, same summary truth, and same first question after settle.

preparedTasks.title: Attach conversation-first origin to Understanding and downstream continuity
liveClosureProof: The live Understanding surface now preserves the same CRM / follow-up project truth that started on the conversation-first front door instead of regressing to generic downstream copy.
The live Loop, Proof, and Timeline surfaces now restore the same CRM / follow-up artifact continuity from that origin under direct downstream navigation and refresh-safe compact route state.

The live Understanding continue CTA now exposes a stateful `/loop?nexusState=...` route, and opening that exact visible route produces the correct CRM / follow-up loop surface on `http://127.0.0.1:4011/`.

preparedTasks.title: Transition conversation-first entry into entry-to-live-skeleton continuity
liveClosureProof: The live `loop` route on `http://127.0.0.1:4011/loop?...` now shows a visible CRM / follow-up first skeleton on screen instead of stopping at hidden planning or summary text.

The live `proof` route on `http://127.0.0.1:4011/proof?...` restores the same CRM / follow-up-specific skeleton continuity with class-aware stats, client list, next action, and generated message content.

Onboarding continuation payloads are now class-aware across landing-page, follow-up dashboard, internal ops dashboard, commerce ops dashboard, and mobile app families instead of collapsing to a generic preview shell.

preparedTasks.title: Isolate fresh project identity and kill shared project reuse across new conversations
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-03 lead-management field-test on http://127.0.0.1:4011/?qaReset=1 still showed stale courier-app residue before the new lead-management idea was entered.

2026-06-04 visible Chrome proof loaded http://127.0.0.1:4011/?qa=1&qaReset=1 with stale courier-project qaState and stored create residue; the visible Create screen returned to a clean empty composer and did not render courier copy, courier project id, old assistant transcript, or inherited project snapshot truth.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if a fresh lead-management start cannot inherit courier-app identity, copy, project id, artifact expectation, or old assistant question state.

preparedTasks.title: Guest/auth session project isolation bridge
preparedTasks.canonicalPlacement: Inserted after W4-FIX-001 and before W4-FIX-005 because clean restore cannot be trusted until project ownership is scoped to an explicit authenticated user, guest session, or local workspace owner.
preparedTasks.scopeBoundary: This is not full first-release auth. ID-001 remains the broader login/registration/logout/account-session boundary. This task owns the minimum session/project isolation bridge required to stop cross-project leakage before server-backed restore.
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-04 visible Chrome test showed Create -> /loop handoff for a lead-management project, then refresh with qaReset=1 still in the URL reset the project to qa-preview-project and rendered a generic landing-page skeleton. That proves route restore can still accept stale/demo state instead of enforcing the active project/session boundary.

2026-06-04 live API proof on http://127.0.0.1:4011 created a fresh local user, verified unauthenticated /api/projects returns 401, verified the fresh user sees zero existing projects, and verified direct restore of onboarding-continuation-preview returns 404 for that user.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if every fresh project is persisted with a stable owner/session scope before W4-FIX-005 short-route restore is trusted.
Pass only if direct route restore checks project id against the active owner/session scope.
Pass only if two sequential projects in one browser cannot inherit each other's class, copy, runtime skeleton, Build rail messages, mutation history, or product-domain state.
Fail if QA state, URL state, localStorage, or a process-global currentProjectId can replace the active project after handoff.

preparedTasks.title: Flush stale hidden brief, summary, placeholder, and family residue on fresh start and explicit correction
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-03 lead-management field-test required correction away from stale courier-app truth before the agent reached the right internal-tool understanding.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if explicit correction to the lead-management internal tool flushes stale family residue from visible copy, hidden brief, summary, placeholder, and downstream skeleton inputs.

preparedTasks.title: Gate family reasoning behind confidence, grounded evidence, and synthesized referents
Add canonical dual-audience and multi-actor product modeling
Replace full-state URL serialization with short route identity and server-backed restore
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-03 lead-management loop restore still depended on a multi-thousand-character nexusState URL payload.

2026-06-04 live browser proof on http://127.0.0.1:4011/loop?qa=1&projectId=<fresh-project>&qaState=<stale-qa-preview> restored the fresh backend project, removed qaState/nexusState from the URL, did not render stale QA project text, and saved screenshot /private/tmp/nexus-w4-fix-005-short-route-live.png.
passFailAddendum: Passed because workspace routes now persist short projectId URLs, direct loop restore prefers explicit projectId over stored QA/browser state, and backend project truth is loaded from /api/projects/<id>.

preparedTasks.title: Make the companion truthful, scoped to the canonical brief, and safely interactive
Add convergence guardrails and a fast path from corrected understanding to the first on-class skeleton
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-03 lead-management field-test reached enough understanding and generated an on-class skeleton, but the visible create screen stayed disabled in a preparing-skeleton state and the skeleton was reachable only after manually opening the loop rail.

2026-06-04 live regression on http://127.0.0.1:4011/?qa=1&qaReset=1 with the lead-management internal-tool idea reached lastAgentDecision.nextMove=advance-to-skeleton and skeletonReady.ready=true, but after 90 seconds the visible app still had screen=create, runtimeTask=null, runtimeClass=null, and the body stayed on 'מכין שלד...'. Screenshot: /private/tmp/nexus-auto-transition-live.png.

2026-06-04 W4-FIX-007 live handoff patch proved automatic transition from 'מכין שלד...' to /loop without manual navigation: final run answered one legitimate clarification, reached 'מכין שלד...' at wait second 44, and reached screen=loop at wait second 45 with runtimeTask=SLICE-005, runtimeClass=internal-tool, runtimeShell=workspace-state-shell, createHidden=true, loopHidden=false. Screenshots: /private/tmp/nexus-w4-fix-007-final-page.png and /private/tmp/nexus-w4-fix-007-runtime-skeleton-element.png.

2026-06-04 visible Chrome test on http://127.0.0.1:4011/?qa=1&qaReset=1 reconfirmed automatic Create -> /loop handoff for the lead-management internal-tool idea, rendered a visible internal-tool runtime skeleton, and kept the Build rail visible. The same run found W4-FIX-005 restore debt: refresh with qaReset=1 still in the URL reset the project to qa-preview-project and rendered a generic landing-page skeleton instead of the lead-management workspace. Screenshot: /private/tmp/nexus-live-visible-leads-refresh-regression.png.

2026-06-06 W4-FIX-007 repair pass found a deeper restore contradiction: the automatic handoff could open /loop with projectId=project-draft, but /api/projects/project-draft returned 404 after refresh. The code now requires /finish to return a canonical project before visible /loop routing, and project-service accepts a discovery-agent advance-to-skeleton decision as build-ready project truth instead of leaving the old completion evaluator as the only gate. Focused tests pass, but a live end-to-end rerun did not reach a stable loop restore before timeout, so W4-FIX-007 remains partial rather than trueGreen.

2026-06-07 visible Chrome proof passed: the lead-management internal-tool idea moved automatically from Create / 'מכין שלד...' to http://127.0.0.1:4011/loop?qa=1&projectId=project-1780838528342-1 without manual navigation, removed qaState/nexusState/qaReset from the route, rendered runtimeTask=SLICE-005 with runtimeProjectId=project-1780838528342-1, and after refresh restored the same loop skeleton from /api/projects/project-1780838528342-1. Evidence: /private/tmp/nexus-w4-fix-007-live-summary.json, /private/tmp/nexus-w4-fix-007-live-before-refresh-proof.png, /private/tmp/nexus-w4-fix-007-live-after-refresh-proof.png.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if enough-understanding / skeleton-ready state automatically opens the visible loop skeleton without manual downstream navigation.
Fail if the composer remains disabled in a preparing-skeleton state while the generated skeleton exists off-screen.
partialClosureEvidence: web/app.js now opens the visible loop runtime skeleton from discovery truth when the product or visual skeleton agents are slow or unavailable, while preserving those agents as hidden engines.

src/core/project-service.js now lets finishOnboardingSession create canonical project truth when the discovery agent has nextMove=advance-to-skeleton and skeletonReady.ready=true, preventing a split between agent build truth and old completion-evaluator blocking.
web/app.js now refuses to route to /loop when /finish does not return a real project id, preventing a non-restorable projectDraftId route from masquerading as a completed Build handoff.
src/core/onboarding-service.js now prevents same-millisecond onboarding session id collisions by appending a monotonic sequence to session ids.
src/core/project-service.js now turns generic project-draft finishes into stable project ids derived from the onboarding session while preserving explicit project ids.
scripts/verify-w4-fix-007-live-proof.mjs now provides repeatable visible-browser proof for Create -> /loop?projectId=<real-project-id> -> refresh restore.
test/loop-core-screen-render.test.js covers W4-FIX-007 fallback runtime skeleton rendering from discovery truth.
test/project-service.test.js covers W4-FIX-007 project-truth finish when the discovery agent approved the skeleton handoff.
test/project-service.test.js covers generic project-draft sessions finishing to distinct restorable project ids.
node scripts/verify-w4-fix-007-live-proof.mjs passed in visible Chrome.

preparedTasks.title: Reach the first on-class skeleton within competitor-parity speed on the Sela regression case
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-03 lead-management field-test is a secondary speed regression because the skeleton existed but did not become visible automatically inside the live wait window.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if the lead-management regression also reaches a first on-class visible skeleton without manual route intervention inside the same stopwatch discipline.

preparedTasks.title: Runtime skeleton backend truth binding
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-04 investigation found the current runtime skeleton was derived in the frontend Build adapter from product and visual skeleton envelopes; no stable backend/project runtime skeleton envelope was found before this task.
2026-06-04 implementation added runtimeSkeletonTruth to canonical project truth at root, context, and state, with stable project/runtime/artifact ids.

2026-06-04 live proof opened /loop?projectId=runtime-truth-live-002&qaReset=1 with no qaState/nexusState and rendered data-runtime-project-id=runtime-truth-live-002, data-runtime-truth-task=RUNTIME-TRUTH-001, and data-runtime-artifact-build-id=runtime-build:runtime-truth-live-002:first-skeleton.
2026-06-04 live refresh preserved the same backend-backed runtime truth dataset.
passFailAddendum: Passed because first runtime skeleton creation now writes canonical Nexus project truth with stable project/runtime/artifact ids.
Passed because the visible Build route can restore from backend project truth through a short projectId route without a giant URL state blob.
This does not close PRODUCT-BACKEND-SKEL-001, because generated product-domain/backend logic is still a separate blocker.

preparedTasks.title: Product backend/domain skeleton
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-04 investigation found product skeleton data objects and actions as descriptive envelopes, but no generated product-domain model, operations, local/mock persistence, or UI-to-domain operation boundary behind the runtime shell.
The current mobile runtime controls render as buttons in the Build surface, but they are not wired to generated product-domain operations.

2026-06-04 implementation added generated product-domain skeleton envelopes with models, operations, mock/local state, persistence boundary, and operation application for mobile app, landing page, internal tool, commerce, game, and generic tool families.

2026-06-04 implementation embedded productDomainSkeleton and productDomainSkeletonId in runtimeSkeletonTruth, persisted productDomainSkeleton at project root/context/state, exposed it through serialization, and rendered data-product-domain-task, data-product-domain-skeleton-id, data-product-domain-kind, data-product-domain-state, and data-product-domain-operation in Build.

2026-06-04 live browser proof rendered /loop with data-product-domain-task=PRODUCT-BACKEND-SKEL-001, product-domain:product-domain-live-proof:internal-tool, workflow-record-local-state, operation buttons, and domain state trace. Screenshot: /private/tmp/nexus-product-domain-skeleton-live.png.
passFailAddendum: Passed because generated products now include product-domain models, operations, mock/local state transitions, and persistence boundaries.
Passed because project truth stores productDomainSkeleton and the visible Build surface binds runtime controls to generated product-domain operations.
Passed because tests prove mobile task creation/update, landing lead validation/submission, internal-tool status update, and commerce cart state through domain operations.
This does not close RUNTIME-SKEL-001, BUILD-MUTATION-TRUTH-001, BLD-AGT-001, production backend deployment, real provider connections, payments, publishing, or release/export.

preparedTasks.title: Interactive product runtime skeleton standard
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-04 user live review found that a phone frame and visual mobile preview are not enough; the skeleton must behave like a simulated product with navigation, local state, and visible interaction changes.
The same standard applies across product classes: mobile app, landing page, internal tool, game, software/tool, and commerce/product site.

2026-06-04 implementation added visible runtime live-state markers, mobile screen navigation, runtime score/state markers, and product-domain operation controls for mobile app, landing page, internal tool, game, commerce, and tool shells.

2026-06-04 implementation added client-side runtime interaction handling for data-runtime-screen-nav and data-product-domain-operation, updating visible runtime state, active mobile screen, interaction count, last operation, score/list state where relevant.

2026-06-04 live mobile proof clicked task.create and screen navigation inside /loop; DOM changed to interactionCount=2, lastOperation=task.create, activeTitle=סיכום, and liveState=עבר למסך: סיכום. Screenshot: /private/tmp/nexus-runtime-skel-mobile-interaction.png.

2026-06-04 live internal-tool proof clicked record.create inside /loop; DOM changed to interactionCount=1, lastOperation=record.create, liveState=פעולה בוצעה: record.create (1), and list item עודכן דרך record.create. Screenshot: /private/tmp/nexus-runtime-skel-internal-interaction.png.
passFailAddendum: Passed because tests prove mobile, landing page, internal tool, game, commerce, and software/tool shells expose interactive state and product-domain operations.
Passed because live browser proof shows mobile screen/state changes and internal-tool list/state changes without manual route hacks.
This does not close BUILD-MUTATION-TRUTH-001, BLD-AGT-001, release/export, production backend deployment, real providers, payments, publishing, or App Store builds.

preparedTasks.title: Build agent frontend/backend/domain mutation contract
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-04 investigation found the Build rail submit path only appends the user message to local companion conversation state and rerenders; it does not call a backend Build agent route or create a mutation intent.

Existing MUT-001 and BLD-AGT-001 contracts require mutation and routing truth, but no current binding guarantees frontend runtime, Nexus project truth, generated product-domain truth, and history/timeline mutation records move together.

2026-06-04 implementation added canonical Build mutation intent/history envelopes, project-service application through product-domain operations, POST /api/projects/:projectId/build-mutations, serialized restore truth, and Build surface mutation truth anchors.

2026-06-04 live Build surface proof opened /loop in QA mode with mutation truth and found data-build-mutation-task=BUILD-MUTATION-TRUTH-001, data-build-mutation-status=applied, data-build-mutation-operation=record.updateStatus, data-build-mutation-history-count=1, and a non-empty mutation id. Screenshot: /private/tmp/nexus-build-mutation-truth-live.png.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if Build changes create mutation intents tied to project, runtime skeleton, and product-domain skeleton before visible success.
Fail if Build agent changes are frontend-only, transcript-only, or lost after refresh.

2026-06-04 passed: node --test test/build-mutation-truth.test.js; node --test --test-name-pattern 'RUNTIME-TRUTH-001|PRODUCT-BACKEND-SKEL-001' test/project-service.test.js; node --test --test-name-pattern 'RUNTIME-SKEL-001|SLICE-005|W4-FIX-007' test/loop-core-screen-render.test.js; node --test test/product-domain-skeleton.test.js.

Closure boundary: this does not close Build rail agent liveliness, natural-language mutation planning, visible agent response, general mutation generation, learning events, release/export, rollback UI, or production backend deployment.

preparedTasks.title: Connect learning layer to runtime skeleton and Build mutations
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-04 investigation found learning contracts and decision-impact code connected to onboarding, generation, release, deployment, continuation, rerun, approval, and failure signals, but not runtime skeleton creation, generated product-domain skeleton creation, Build mutation intents, or product-domain operation outcomes.

2026-06-07 implementation added runtime learning events for runtime_skeleton.created, product_domain_skeleton.created, build_agent_request.received, build_mutation_intent.created, build_mutation_outcome.applied/failed, and product_domain_operation outcome events.

2026-06-07 live API proof on http://127.0.0.1:4011 signed up runtime-learning-user-1780839167438, created runtime-learning-live-1780839167438, confirmed runtime/domain learning events after create, applied record.updateStatus through /api/projects/:projectId/build-mutations, and confirmed restored project truth includes six LEARNING-RUNTIME-001 event types with mayOverwriteProjectTruth=false.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if learning events are emitted from runtime skeleton, product-domain skeleton, Build request, mutation intent, and mutation outcome flows with stable ids.
Fail if learning remains onboarding/planning-only while claiming runtime/build intelligence.

Passed because tests and live API verification proved runtime, product-domain, Build request, mutation intent, mutation outcome, and product-domain operation outcome events are stored with stable project/runtime/domain/mutation/outcome ids.

Closure boundary: this does not close BLD-AGT-001 natural-language routing, visible mutation planning, Visual Build Agent routing, Verification / QA Agent routing, Release Agent routing, rollback UI, or production backend deployment.

preparedTasks.title: Make the first visible skeleton deepen progressively and explain why this shape exists
liveFieldTestEvidence: 2026-06-03 lead-management skeleton preserved no-WhatsApp-integration and no-automation boundaries, but exposed internal or mistranslated trust-breaking copy and too much planning-document language.
passFailAddendum: Pass only if the lead-management skeleton feels like a usable internal lead workspace with lead list, status, owner, reminder, and next step.
Pass only if relevant learning signals can influence skeleton choice or interaction emphasis without silently changing project truth.
Fail if first skeleton copy exposes internal/debug language, mistranslated labels, or planning-document framing instead of a live workspace.
Fail if stored learning exists but skeleton choice still behaves as if no prior runtime or mutation outcome was known.

preparedTasks.title: Beat competitor first-artifact quality inside the same time band
Implement feedback-driven product mutation loop
passFailAddendum: Pass only if feedback-driven product changes use relevant learning signals as bounded working instructions before choosing a mutation path.
Fail if a failed change is presented as success.
Fail if learning remains stored but does not influence the visible product mutation loop.

nextCanonicalExecutionTask.title: Claim-to-visible-proof execution integrity gate
nextCanonicalExecutionTask.reason: SURFACE-OWNER-RUNTIME-001 closed trueGreen on 2026-06-13 with a route/surface/runtime ownership map, live DOM ownership markers, targeted tests, and live browser proof. LIVE-PROOF-INTEGRITY-001 is now the earliest release-blocker because it consumes that ownership map to prevent future false fixed/trueGreen claims.

completedCanonicalExecutionTasks.title: Server-verified session identity boundary

laneDefinitions.name: Design Runtime Foundations
laneDefinitions.purpose: Create the boundary, capability, reliability, and consistency foundations required before higher-order Wave 3 generation and adaptive loops.

laneDefinitions.name: Nexus Core Product Experience & Surface Delivery
laneDefinitions.purpose: Own the live Nexus product shell, route/navigation surfaces, unified home dashboard, guided execution surfaces, first-user experience, and the backend-to-frontend bridge that turns canonical state into usable product screens.

laneDefinitions.name: Existing Business Intake & Diagnosis
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn uploaded assets, repositories, live sites, and analytics exports into normalized current-state diagnosis and executable import-and-continue plans.

laneDefinitions.name: Persistence & Return Continuity
laneDefinitions.purpose: Replace memory-only user, project, and session continuity with durable persistence and reliable return-tomorrow product behavior.

laneDefinitions.name: Behavioral Analytics & User Truth
laneDefinitions.purpose: Replace heuristic analytics with durable history, true user attribution, and retention truth.

laneDefinitions.name: Autonomous Product Ops
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn post-execution evidence into measurable feedback, goal evaluation, and adaptive optimization loops.

laneDefinitions.name: External Capability Fabric
laneDefinitions.purpose: Own the registry, credential binding, webhook intake, provider health, and external-tool bridges that let Nexus connect safely to repos, providers, and imported design assets.

laneDefinitions.name: Execution Surface / Real Execution
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn canonical execution intent into real provider, IDE-agent, and artifact-producing execution with strict routing, session, invocation, and result truth.

laneDefinitions.name: Deployment Reality
laneDefinitions.purpose: Turn execution outputs into real deployment attempts and canonical deployment evidence instead of assuming release side effects.

laneDefinitions.name: Live Release Verification
laneDefinitions.purpose: Validate real post-deploy production health and release readiness before Wave 3 can claim live launch truth.

laneDefinitions.name: AI Generation Engine
laneDefinitions.purpose: Establish the request, provider, service, execution, and normalization pipeline for controlled AI-generated design proposals.

laneDefinitions.name: AI Observability & Safety Ops
laneDefinitions.purpose: Measure generation health, provider reliability, prompt-contract failures, and review acceptance so AI output is governed by runtime truth rather than hopeful tests.

laneDefinitions.name: Owner Creative Control Plane & Generated Surface Proof
laneDefinitions.purpose: Prove generated surfaces are renderable, accessible, on-brand, reviewable, editable, attributable, and safely integrated.

laneDefinitions.name: Growth & Monetization Expansion
laneDefinitions.purpose: Expand Wave 2 billing foundations into true self-serve monetization state and lifecycle actions.

externalSatisfiedPrerequisites.name: Build & Release System
externalSatisfiedPrerequisites.note: Wave 2 still carries unresolved build/deploy cost truth. Wave 3 billing expansion is scoped to self-serve state and lifecycle, not full build/deploy monetization truth.

Merged into preview, diff, and review handoff tasks.
Removed from initial Wave 3 canon; regeneration belongs after first safe end-to-end design proof exists.

dependencyRepairs.reason: Repository already exposes canonical observability-derived reliability inputs through context-builder.
Go-to-market is a planning lane label, not an executable prerequisite.

repairedTo: optional extension registry when it exists
dependencyRepairs.reason: Wave 3 must be able to start with internal capability truth instead of waiting on a non-canonical lane label.
The external integration label is not a task; the real executable dependency is a strict provider adapter contract.

dependencyRepairs.taskName: Create billing plan catalog and selection model
dependencyRepairs.reason: Wave 2 already ships enough billing state to start self-serve plan selection.

repairedFrom: Create system bottleneck detector (stale doc status)
dependencyRepairs.reason: The detector already exists in repo; only the consistency validator and evaluation schema remain unfinished.

dependencyRepairs.taskName: Nexus Core Product Experience & Surface Delivery
dependencyRepairs.reason: The repository already has fragmented screen/runtime primitives and isolated surface models, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for composing them into the live Nexus product experience.
The repo has provider health and review primitives, but no canonical Wave 3 lane owned prompt failures, provider latency/failure metrics, generation success, or acceptance truth.

The repo already accepts files, links, and connected repositories, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for converting imported business assets into diagnosis, executable tasks, and continue-from-current-state plans.
The repo already exposes persistence contracts and some durable analytics history, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for durable users, projects, sessions, and true next-day continuity.

repairedFrom: provider primitives spread across backlog lanes
dependencyRepairs.reason: The repo already contains fragmented connector/runtime primitives, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for binding credentials, inbound provider events, source-control connectivity, or cross-provider health into a coherent product-facing fabric.

proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 lacked canonical ownership for the authenticated Nexus shell even though repo reality already includes auth screens, workspace navigation, companion surfaces, and owner-facing dashboards.
Backend state already reaches isolated views, but no Wave 3 task owns assembling the live authenticated product shell.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create Nexus navigation and route surface binder
proactiveAdditions.reason: Backend-to-frontend route binding exists only as scattered runtime/view models; Wave 3 needed an explicit bridge owner.
Repo reality has owner and analytics surfaces, but no canonical Wave 3 task owns the default home entry surface for an authenticated Nexus user.
Wave 3 needs a single owner for the daily work surface that binds companion, recommendations, monitoring, and task state into a real user-facing workspace.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create settings and profile surface model
proactiveAdditions.reason: Account/profile/settings state exists in fragments, but no Wave 3 task owns rendering it as a canonical Nexus product surface.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create AI control center and generated surface delivery binder
proactiveAdditions.reason: The plan previously generated proposals without assigning ownership for surfacing accepted/generated Nexus screens inside the product itself.
The repo has fragmented workspace summaries, owner dashboards, and analytics panels, but no single Wave 3 owner for the main logged-in wow-value home.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create today priorities and next action feed
proactiveAdditions.reason: A product-grade home needs an explicit owner for priorities today and next best actions instead of relying on scattered roadmap panels.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create owner visibility strip for home dashboard
proactiveAdditions.reason: Owner visibility exists in operational dashboards, but Wave 3 lacked a product-facing summary layer that brings it into the main home experience.
The repo can orchestrate tasks and show queues, but there was no canonical Wave 3 owner for a focused task-doing experience.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create task step flow and progress binder
proactiveAdditions.reason: Product-grade execution requires a step-by-step task flow bound to real progress state, not just background cycle status.
Approvals already exist in runtime state, but Wave 3 lacked a user-facing execution handoff surface when a task needs approval.
Wave 3 needed runtime truth for prompt failures, provider latency, generation success, review acceptance, and rejection causes.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create provider latency and failure tracker
proactiveAdditions.reason: Provider health primitives already exist, but Wave 3 had no canonical tracker for design-generation provider reliability.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create generation success and acceptance tracker
proactiveAdditions.reason: Generated output cannot be governed truthfully without measuring produced proposals, accepted proposals, rejected proposals, and owner/operator acceptance.
Prompt contract doctrine in the permanent orchestrator needs a concrete telemetry owner in the canonical execution map.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create AI generation review dashboard model
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 needs a surface that turns AI-generation telemetry into operator-visible truth instead of burying failures in low-level runtime state.
Wave 3 adds generated UI surfaces. Safe execution requires a canonical proof contract for accessibility, performance, brand, provenance, and renderability evidence.
Generated surfaces need accessibility proof before they can be marked green.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create generated surface performance budget validator
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 needs real render/performance protection, not only structural validation.
Generated surfaces must respect product tokens and positioning, not merely render.
Wave 3 needs explicit ownership/history for accepted generated assets and proposals.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create uploaded intake to scanner handoff
proactiveAdditions.reason: The repo already stores onboarding uploads and has a project scanner, but the bridge from imported assets to diagnostic scanning was still missing and blocks true import-and-continue onboarding.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create existing business asset normalization layer
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 needs a canonical intake layer that turns mixed files, links, repositories, and exports into normalized imported business evidence instead of treating them as loose attachments.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create repository import and codebase diagnosis bridge
proactiveAdditions.reason: Git connectivity and project scanning exist, but no canonical Wave 3 task owns turning connected repositories into a current-state diagnosis flow.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create live website ingestion and funnel diagnosis model
proactiveAdditions.reason: Wave 3 lacked any owner for crawling an existing business website and turning it into structural UX/funnel diagnosis.
Analytics dashboards exist, but there is no canonical owner for importing external analytics exports and converting them into trusted product/growth signals.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create imported asset task extraction module
proactiveAdditions.reason: Imported files, sites, analytics, and repositories must become executable tasks rather than passive reference material.
Wave 3 needs a product path for continuing from an existing business reality instead of restarting every user from a greenfield journey.

Wave 3 needs one canonical owner for converting validated learning, feedback, and optimization outputs into real canonical tasks with execution_order and dependency truth instead of sidecar roadmap suggestions.
Wave 3 needs a canonical contract for which user, session, project, and workspace records must survive restart and power true return-tomorrow continuity.
Signup/login exists, but user identity currently remains process-bound instead of being product-grade durable state.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create durable project and workspace store
proactiveAdditions.reason: Projects and workspaces need durable persistence beyond process memory for a real product experience.
Return-tomorrow continuity needs durable session truth instead of ephemeral in-memory auth/session state.
Wave 3 needs an explicit owner for restoring a user to the right project, screen, and next action after restart or next-day return.
Wave 3 needed a canonical owner for describing which external systems Nexus can bind to, under what auth modes, scopes, and operational guarantees.
Git connectivity exists in isolation, but no canonical Wave 3 task owns binding repository state into live Nexus import and continuation flows.
Encrypted credential storage exists, but Wave 3 still lacked a canonical secret-resolution owner needed for real provider connectivity.
Provider contracts and encrypted credentials exist separately, but Wave 3 had no canonical owner for binding usable provider credentials into runtime connectors safely.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create inbound provider webhook ingestion gateway
proactiveAdditions.reason: Provider events can only become product truth when external webhook deliveries are normalized into canonical inbound runtime state.

proactiveAdditions.taskName: Create external provider health and failover orchestrator
proactiveAdditions.reason: Provider degradation and recovery primitives exist, but no single Wave 3 owner governs connector-level degraded-mode behavior across imported external capabilities.
Wave 3 AI/design flows need a real bridge for imported design assets instead of relying on generic file uploads and Figma-like attachment heuristics.
Wave 3 needs a canonical action-routing contract before external execution can move from UI state into real provider actions.
Provider selection and execution-surface routing cannot remain implicit once Nexus starts dispatching real actions.
Wave 3 needs an owned bridge that turns canonical execution plans into real provider invocations instead of stopping at planning state.
Local and IDE-based coding executors need a strict contract before they can participate in controlled Wave 3 execution.
Wave 3 needs a canonical adapter for safe local coding-agent execution instead of ad hoc external agent assumptions.
Execution providers must expose normalized capabilities before Nexus can route or constrain real execution truthfully.
Real provider execution needs canonical session lifecycle ownership for resume, cancel, and cross-provider correlation.
External executor results must be normalized into one Nexus runtime shape before they can update state safely.
Wave 3 needs a canonical invocation envelope for real execution attempts, approval posture, and expected side effects.
Real execution must collect artifacts, diffs, logs, and previews as first-class evidence instead of side effects.
Wave 3 needs a single owned execution-result surface that downstream validation, deployment, and feedback loops can trust.
The execution path still lacks a canonical owner for turning prepared release artifacts into a real deployment action.
Deployment truth is incomplete without an owned evidence collector for endpoints, environment identifiers, and provider metadata.
Deployment output needs a canonical normalized result surface before release truth can be validated safely.
Wave 3 needs a post-deploy health gate that proves launch reality instead of assuming deployment success implies release readiness.
Users need a canonical launch-confirmation state that reflects real post-deploy evidence rather than provider optimism.
Release truth needs a canonical evaluator that combines validation, approvals, deployment, and production-health evidence.

wave3OrderedExecutionMap.blocker: Turn Zero downgrade: Wave 2 billing foundations exist, but no repository implementation, direct tests, or downstream billingPlanSelectionModel consumers exist yet; inherited in-progress state was placeholder progress and this is not the current lowest executable task.

Turn Zero downgrade: normalized billing events and billing state exist, but no repository implementation, direct tests, or downstream paymentMethodState consumers exist yet; current billing screen tests explicitly prove the field is absent.

Turn Zero downgrade: normalized billing events and billing state exist, but no repository implementation, direct tests, or downstream billingDetailsState consumers exist yet; current billing screen tests explicitly prove the field is absent.

Turn Zero downgrade: subscription and billing evidence inputs exist, but no repository implementation, direct tests, or downstream invoiceState consumers exist yet; current billing screen tests explicitly prove invoices are absent.
